title: Store multiple images in single tar file
body:
Hi!
I'm looking for a way to store multiple container images in one tar
Ideally it should create a tar just like microk8s does with:
```shell
microk8s images export-local > images.tar
``` 

i've tried to achieve that with:
```shell
skopeo copy docker://busybox:latest oci:/tmp/all_images:busybox:latest
skopeo copy docker://hello-world:latest oci:/tmp/all_images:hello-world:latest
``` 
but in /tmp/all_images the manifest.json file is missing
```shell
tree /tmp/all_images/
/tmp/all_images/
|-- blobs
|   `-- sha256
|       |-- 4be429a5fbb2e71ae7958bfa558bc637cf3a61baf40a708cb8fff532b39e52d0
|       |-- 7b2699543f22d5b8dc8d66a5873eb246767bca37232dee1e7a3b8c9956bceb0c
|       |-- ba5dc23f65d4cc4a4535bce55cf9e63b068eb02946e3422d3587e8ce803b6aab
|       |-- c1ec31eb59444d78df06a974d155e597c894ab4cda84f08294145e845394988e
|       |-- d2c94e258dcb3c5ac2798d32e1249e42ef01cba4841c2234249495f87264ac5a
|       `-- e2fc4e5012d16e7fe466f5291c476431beaa1f9b90a5c2125b493ed28e2aba57
|-- index.json
`-- oci-layout

2 directories, 8 files
``` 

while the ctr's archive structure is:
```shell
tree /tmp/all_images/
//tmp/all_images/
├── blobs
│   └── sha256
│       ├── 0076c703494a555d10a14e1091c2b9a3b83a2cbfe0a2059a55266c7e1a1378ce
....
│       └── ff478f20468cc6628cf50581355deee52fe0b956056920ff40074033025f0f07
├── index.json
├── manifest.json
└── oci-layout

3 directories, 300 files
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

The first version looks plausible. The OCI image specification does not specify any role for a `manifest.json` file name — in fact it would be _exactly what you don’t want_: if there are multiple images in a single OCI layout, but only one `manifest.json` file path, that path could only contain a manifest of one of the images.

In general, using `oci:` and tarring it up manually is a reasonable approach. (There was some work to directly create an `oci-archive:` with multiple images, but that has not made progress recently.)

Ultimately, Skopeo is a fairly thin wrapper: operations like creating a single archive file out of multiple sources can, when implemented, be done in the underlying containers/image library, but doing that would require writing (not too much) custom Go code.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if there are any outstanding questions.
